column2: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Tesla, Amazon, Exxon, Sunrun, Enphase, SoFi, Datadog, and More Stocks traded mostly to the downside Tuesday after President Donald Trump extended the date when his so-called reciprocal tariffs would take effect to Aug. 1 from July 9. Will Paramount Stay in the S&P 500? Here’s an Update On the Situation. Skydance Media is set to close on its deal to purchase a controlling interest in  Paramount Global  on Thursday raising questions about whether the company will remain in  S&P Dow Jones Indices hasn’t announced any replacement for Paramount, and it’s possible it will remain right where it is.  Many investors assumed that Paramount would be dropped from the index due to its small market capitalization, opening the way for potential additions like Robinhood Markets or AppLovin.  After the deal, the market value of Paramount, which owns CBS, a movie studio, a group of cable networks, and operates a streaming service, should be around $11 billion, half the current minimum for new entrants into the index of $22.7 billion.
